question: What's the best time to visit Newcastle NSW with kids?
answer: G'day! Spring (September-November) and autumn (March-May) are absolutely brilliant for families. You'll get gorgeous weather without the summer crowds, and accommodation's more affordable. Summer's magic too, but book early for school holidays! Winter's mild here, perfect if your little ones love spotting whales migrating past our stunning coastline. The beaches and parks are enjoyable year-round, mate.

question: Are there pet friendly holiday parks in Newcastle NSW?
answer: Too right there are! Many Newcastle holiday parks welcome your furry family members. Most pet-friendly parks require dogs to be on-leash and have designated areas. You'll typically pay a small fee per pet per night. Always ring ahead to confirm their pet policy and any size restrictions. Some have fully fenced sites which are bonzer for keeping pups safe while the kids play.

question: How much do holiday parks cost in Newcastle NSW during school holidays?
answer: During school holidays, expect to pay $150-$300 per night for powered sites, and $180-$450 for cabins, depending on size and facilities. Peak summer holidays (Christmas-January) are priciest. Book at least 3-6 months ahead for decent prices and availability. Off-peak rates can be 30-40% cheaper, so consider travelling during term time if you can, legends!

question: What should I pack for a holiday park stay with young kids?
answer: Don't forget the basics, mate! Pack swimmers, sunscreen (SPF50+), hats, insect repellent, and beach toys. Bring their favourite bedtime items, a basic first-aid kit, and plenty of snacks. Most parks have BBQs, so bring tongs and cleaning supplies. A portable fan's handy for summer nights. Chuck in bikes, scooters, or outdoor games too—there's usually heaps of space to explore!

question: What is there to do near Newcastle NSW holiday parks for families?
answer: You're spoilt for choice! The beaches are stunning—Nobbys, Bar, and Newcastle Beach are tops for families. Visit Blackbutt Reserve for free wildlife encounters, explore Fort Scratchley, or ride the historic tram. Kids love the ocean baths and playgrounds scattered everywhere. Grab fish and chips at the harbour, and don't miss the weekend markets. There's something for every age, guaranteed!
